reviewNote: Editorial mappings pending sign-off by a qualified compliance SME. EQUIVALENT edges in particular must be reviewed before customers rely on cross-framework propagation.

edges.rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) information system security policy is implemented for digital-infrastructure entities by CIR 2024/2690 Annex 3.1 security policy requirement.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) security roles and responsibilities map one-to-one to CIR Annex 3.2 roles and responsibilities for network and information system security.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk assessment policy corresponds to CIR Annex 4.2 risk assessment methodology and criteria.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures are specified by CIR Annex 5.1 incident handling policy and procedures.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident detection overlaps CIR Annex 5.2 monitoring and logging, but CIR adds explicit logging duties.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity planning corresponds to CIR Annex 6.1 business continuity and disaster recovery plans.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to CIR Annex 6.2 backup management and data restoration.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment corresponds to CIR Annex 7.1 supply chain security policy.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) contractual security requirements correspond to CIR Annex 7.2 supplier security requirements in contracts.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) vulnerability handling corresponds to CIR Annex 8.1 vulnerability handling and disclosure.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) security awareness training corresponds to CIR Annex 9.1 awareness and training programmes.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) cryptographic policy corresponds to CIR Annex 10.1 use of cryptography and encryption.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) access control policy corresponds to CIR Annex 11.1 access control policies and procedures.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) multi-factor authentication deployment corresponds to CIR Annex 11.2 multi-factor authentication.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management and inventory corresponds to CIR Annex 12.1 asset management and inventory.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment policy corresponds to CIR Annex 13.1 security effectiveness assessment and testing.
NIS2 Art.20(1) management approval overlaps DORA Art.5 management body accountability, but DORA is ICT-risk specific with broader duties.
NIS2 Art.20(2) management cybersecurity training corresponds to DORA Art.5(4) management ICT risk training.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk assessment policy overlaps DORA Art.6/8 ICT risk assessment methodology; DORA is ICT-scoped and more prescriptive.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) information system security policy overlaps DORA Art.9(4) ICT security policies, procedures, protocols and tools.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident detection overlaps DORA Art.10 anomalous activity detection; DORA adds alert-threshold specifics.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident classification corresponds to DORA Art.18 ICT-related incident classification.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures overlap DORA Art.17 ICT incident management process; DORA adds financial-sector workflow duties.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) lessons learned corresponds to DORA Art.13 post-incident review and lessons learned.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity plan overlaps DORA Art.11 ICT business continuity policy; DORA is ICT-scoped.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) crisis management corresponds to DORA Art.11(7) crisis management process.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to DORA Art.12 backup policy.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) disaster recovery planning overlaps DORA Art.11(3) ICT response and recovery plans.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) BC/DR testing overlaps DORA Art.11(6) disaster recovery testing; DORA prescribes frequency for critical functions.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) BC/DR testing covers backup restoration testing (DORA Art.12) plus wider exercise scope.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business impact analysis corresponds to DORA Art.11(2) BIA for ICT disruption.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment overlaps DORA Art.28(4) pre-contract due diligence; DORA is contract-lifecycle specific.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) contractual security requirements overlap DORA Art.30 minimum contractual provisions; DORA mandates specific clauses.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier monitoring corresponds to DORA Art.28 ongoing provider risk monitoring.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) secure development lifecycle corresponds to DORA Art.9 secure acquisition, development and maintenance.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) patch management corresponds to DORA Art.9 vulnerability and patch management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) vulnerability assessments correspond to DORA Art.25 vulnerability assessments.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) penetration testing corresponds to DORA Art.25 penetration testing.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) cryptographic policy corresponds to DORA Art.9(2) cryptography and key management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) privileged access management corresponds to DORA Art.9(4)(c) privileged access management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) identity management and access reviews correspond to DORA Art.9(4)(c) identity and access management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management overlaps DORA Art.8(1) ICT asset inventory; DORA is ICT-asset scoped.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) MFA deployment corresponds to DORA Art.9(4)(d) strong authentication for critical systems.
NIS2 Art.23(1) significant incident notification overlaps DORA Art.17/19 detection-to-notification workflow; regimes and recipients differ.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(a) 24h early warning parallels DORA Art.19 major incident reporting workflow; deadlines and templates differ.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(b) 72h notification parallels DORA reporting deadline tracking; different deadline scheme (initial/intermediate/final).
NIS2 Art.23(4)(d) final report parallels DORA standard incident reporting templates; content requirements differ.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) information system security policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.1 policies for information security.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) security roles and responsibilities correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.2.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident classification corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.25 assessment and decision on information security events.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.26 response to information security incidents.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) lessons learned corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.27 learning from information security incidents.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.29 information security during disruption; ISO is security-continuity scoped.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.13 information backup.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) contractual security requirements correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.20 addressing security within supplier agreements.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier monitoring corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.22 monitoring, review and change management of supplier services.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) secure development lifecycle corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.25 secure development life cycle.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) vulnerability handling overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities; NIS2 adds disclosure duties.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) patch management is a subset of ISO 27001 A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) security awareness training corresponds to ISO 27001 A.6.3 awareness, education and training.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) cryptographic policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.24 use of cryptography.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) personnel background checks correspond to ISO 27001 A.6.1 screening.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) access control policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.15 access control.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) privileged access management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.2 privileged access rights.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) identity management and access reviews correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.18 access rights.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management and inventory corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.9 inventory of information and other associated assets.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) MFA deployment overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.5 secure authentication; ISO covers wider authentication controls.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(a) regulatory early warning is related to ISO 27001 A.6.8 internal event reporting but targets authorities, not internal channels.
DORA Art.9(2) cryptography and key management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.24 use of cryptography.
DORA Art.9(4)(c) identity and access management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.16 identity management.
DORA Art.9(4)(c) privileged access management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.2 privileged access rights.
DORA Art.9 vulnerability and patch management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities.
DORA Art.12 backup policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.13 information backup.
DORA Art.17 ICT incident management process corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.24 incident management planning and preparation.
DORA Art.13 post-incident review corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.27 learning from information security incidents.
DORA evidence preservation and forensic readiness corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.28 collection of evidence.
DORA Art.8(1) ICT asset inventory overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.9; ISO also covers information assets beyond ICT.
DORA Art.30 minimum contractual provisions overlap ISO 27001 A.5.20 supplier agreements; DORA mandates sector-specific clauses.
DORA security logging and SIEM overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.15 logging; DORA adds detection use-case duties.
DORA network security and segmentation overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.22 segregation of networks.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk assessment policy overlaps NIST CSF ID.RA-05 risk understanding and prioritization.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident detection overlaps NIST CSF DE.CM-01 network monitoring.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures overlap NIST CSF RS.MA-01 incident response plan execution.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to NIST CSF PR.DS-11 backups created, protected, maintained, and tested.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment overlaps NIST CSF GV.SC-06 supplier due diligence.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) awareness training corresponds to NIST CSF PR.AT-01 personnel awareness and training.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) encryption deployment overlaps NIST CSF PR.DS-01 data-at-rest protection.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) access reviews overlap NIST CSF PR.AA-05 access permissions and least privilege.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) MFA deployment overlaps NIST CSF PR.AA-03 users, services, and hardware are authenticated.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management covers hardware inventories (NIST CSF ID.AM-01) plus software and information assets.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(b) 72h notification overlaps NIST CSF RS.CO-02 stakeholder notification; NIS2 targets a regulator with a fixed deadline.
DORA Art.5 management body accountability corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RR-01 leadership accountability for cybersecurity risk.
DORA ICT risk appetite and tolerance corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RM-02 risk appetite and tolerance statements.
DORA threat intelligence monitoring corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-02 cyber threat intelligence.
DORA Art.18 incident classification corresponds to NIST CSF RS.MA-03 incidents categorized and prioritized.
DORA disaster recovery testing overlaps NIST CSF ID.IM-02 improvements from tests and exercises.
DORA ICT risk register is related to NIST CSF ID.RA-05 risk prioritization as its record-keeping substrate.
DORA ongoing provider risk monitoring corresponds to NIST CSF GV.SC-07 supplier risks understood, assessed and monitored.
